select_when: The number of plausible tasks, issues, or opportunities already exceeds the true execution capacity of the team.
Sequencing matters and explicit non-goals or kill criteria can protect high-leverage work from constant interruption.
The organization must choose what gets to be first, what gets deferred, and what gets eliminated entirely.
avoid_when: Prioritization is becoming endless reshuffling, so the team talks constantly about leverage but rarely commits long enough to realize it.
Urgent, visible work keeps winning by default and the system is quietly starving the slower, higher-value work it claims to care about.

curation_notes.summary: Normalized the model around sequencing and exclusion under capacity constraints rather than donor lists of resource-allocation examples.
donor_drops: Dropped donor examples tied to marketing and prompt-engineering contexts because the raw model already captures the structural overload pattern.
Dropped donor higher-order overlays and kept only first-order operational fields.
open_questions: The output type compresses both ordering and deliberate exclusion into one operational label.

select_when.note: The raw file explicitly defines the overload and sequencing activation surface; donor drafts were narrowed to preserve only those structural triggers.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions are directly supported by explicit raw danger lines.

input_type.note: The source clearly operates on a capacity-overloaded portfolio of work, but the typed label is a reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model drives ordering and explicit non-goals; the output type compresses that into a concise operational artifact label.

reasoning_types.note: The model is diagnostic and causal in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
